<timestamp>Tuesday, Apr 28, 2026, 5:09 PM (UTC+2)</timestamp>
<user_query>
I see several problems:

- The quartz brain is not reflecting the brain (obsidian) in /mnt/c/Users/lgiron/Documents/developer-brain
- the test telegram is marked as successful but I did not receive the message: I think the channel id is missing
</user_query>

I’m going to diagnose both issues end-to-end: first verify the brain sync path/config (Quartz vs Obsidian vault), then inspect the Telegram test flow and scheduler config to confirm whether a channel/chat id is required and currently missing. I’ll apply fixes directly and validate with commands.

content.text: I found the session tooling in place and now I’m moving to the actual failures: I’ll trace where Quartz is sourced from versus your Obsidian vault path, then inspect Telegram test success criteria to verify it wrongly passes without a target channel/chat id.

I’ve identified likely root causes in code paths. Next I’m reading the memory/quartz resolution logic and then I’ll patch two things: enforce a valid Telegram target id for real sends, and make Quartz fallback read from `BRAIN_PATH/projects` when that is the active source.

content.text: I’m now applying the Quartz fix in the memory API layer so Brain pages are read from the external `BRAIN_PATH/projects` when that’s where your Obsidian-backed files actually live, instead of being locked to the instance workspace path.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/lgiron/lab_perso/clawvis/services/kanban/kanban_api/core.py
@@
 def _quartz_public_dir() -> Path | None:
@@
     return None
 
 
+def _fallback_projects_dir() -> Path:
+    primary = active_brain_memory_root() / "projects"
+    if any(primary.glob("*.html")):
+        return primary
+    bp = os.environ.get("BRAIN_PATH", "").strip()
+    if bp:
+        external = Path(bp).expanduser() / "projects"
+        if external.is_dir() and any(external.glob("*.html")):
+            return external
+    return primary
+
+
 def list_memory_quartz_pages() -> dict:
@@
-    projects_dir = active_brain_memory_root() / "projects"
+    projects_dir = _fallback_projects_dir()
     projects_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
     files = sorted(p.name for p in projects_dir.glob("*.html"))
     return {"files": files, "source": "fallback"}
@@
     quartz_dir = _quartz_public_dir()
     if quartz_dir is not None:
         path = quartz_dir / safe
     else:
-        path = active_brain_memory_root() / "projects" / safe
+        path = _fallback_projects_dir() / safe
*** End Patch

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/lgiron/lab_perso/clawvis/README.md
@@
-**Cron + Telegram:** `docker compose up` (and `clawvis restart` in Docker mode) start `telegram` and `scheduler` with the rest of the stack. Without `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, the telegram service runs in **stub mode** (HTTP `/send` only — logs deliveries, no Telegram API). Set `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` and `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` in `.env` for a real bot. Cron definitions: `services/scheduler/definitions/jobs/*.yaml` and workflows in `services/scheduler/definitions/workflows/*.yaml`.
+**Cron + Telegram:** `docker compose up` (and `clawvis restart` in Docker mode) start `telegram` and `scheduler` with the rest of the stack. Without `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, the telegram service runs in **stub mode** (HTTP `/send` only — logs deliveries, no Telegram API). Set `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` and `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` in `.env` for a real bot. `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` must be the target chat/channel id (not the bot id), otherwise `/test` and scheduler notifications are rejected. Cron definitions: `services/scheduler/definitions/jobs/*.yaml` and workflows in `services/scheduler/definitions/workflows/*.yaml`.
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/lgiron/lab_perso/clawvis/CHANGELOG.md
@@
 ## [Unreleased]
 
+### Brain + Telegram — Quartz source alignment and strict notification checks (2026-04-28)
+- `hub-memory-api` now uses `MEMORY_ROOT=${BRAIN_PATH}` in Docker so the Brain/Quartz API tracks the same external Obsidian vault path as the rest of the stack.
+- Quartz fallback page lookup now checks `BRAIN_PATH/projects` when local instance memory has no generated HTML pages.
+- Telegram `/send` and `/test` now reject real-mode delivery when `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` is missing (`412 missing chat_id`).
+- Scheduler now validates Telegram notify HTTP responses (`raise_for_status` + JSON `ok`) before logging `job.notified`, avoiding false “success” when delivery failed.
+
 ### Docker — kanban-api startup permission fix (2026-04-28)
 - **Root cause**: `kanban-api` bind-mounted the full repo at `/clawvis`, reusing host `.venv` files owned by `nobody:nogroup`; `uv run` failed with `Permission denied` while replacing `bin/kanban-api`, so the container exited on boot.
*** End Patch

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /mnt/c/Users/lgiron/Documents/developer-brain/inbox/daily/implementation/clawvis/2026-04-28-kanban-api-startup-fix.md
@@
 ### Result
 - Jobs API now returns `path: /skills/jobs` with all expected entries.
 - Workflows API returns `knowledge-and-innovation` as expected.
+
+---
+
+## Additional updates (telegram + brain reflection)
+
+### Goal
+- Fix two production issues:
+  - Quartz brain did not reflect the external Obsidian vault at `/mnt/c/Users/lgiron/Documents/developer-brain`.
+  - Telegram test/jobs reported success even when no message was delivered.
+
+### What changed
+- `docker-compose.yml`
+  - `hub-memory-api` now runs with `MEMORY_ROOT=${BRAIN_PATH}` so memory/quartz endpoints resolve the same external vault.
+- `services/kanban/kanban_api/core.py`
+  - Added `_fallback_projects_dir()` to use `BRAIN_PATH/projects` for fallback Quartz pages when active instance memory has no HTML.
+- `services/kanban/kanban_api/memory_api.py`
+  - Quartz static resolver now uses the same fallback directory logic.
+- `services/telegram/core/config.py` + `services/telegram/core/bot.py`
+  - Added explicit `chat_id` presence check.
+  - `/send` and `/test` now return `412` with `missing chat_id` when token is present but target chat is not configured.
+  - `/health` now exposes `chat_id_configured`.
+- `services/scheduler/core/scheduler.py`
+  - Telegram notify now checks HTTP status and JSON `{ok:true}` before logging success.
+  - Prevents false positive "job.notified" when Telegram rejected delivery.
+- Docs updated:
+  - `README.md` clarifies `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` must be the destination chat/channel id, not the bot id.
+  - `CHANGELOG.md` updated.
+
+### Commands/tests
+- `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=.uvtmp uv run --directory services/telegram python tests/test_telegram.py` (pass)
+- `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=.uvtmp uv run --directory services/scheduler pytest tests/test_scheduler.py` (fails at import path in existing test harness: `ModuleNotFoundError: scheduler`)
+- `python3 -m py_compile services/scheduler/core/scheduler.py services/telegram/core/bot.py services/telegram/core/config.py services/kanban/kanban_api/core.py services/kanban/kanban_api/memory_api.py` (pass)
+
+### Follow-ups
+- Verify a real `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` (channel/group/user id) in `.env` and restart `telegram` + `scheduler`.
+- Run an end-to-end check with `POST /test` and one manual scheduler job trigger after restart.
*** End Patch

content.text:
Implemented fixes for both issues, directly in the stack.

### What I changed

- **Brain/Quartz source alignment**
  - Updated `docker-compose.yml` so `hub-memory-api` uses `MEMORY_ROOT=${BRAIN_PATH}` (instead of instance workspace path).
  - Added fallback logic in `services/kanban/kanban_api/core.py` so fallback Quartz pages can come from `BRAIN_PATH/projects` when instance memory has no generated HTML.
  - Updated `services/kanban/kanban_api/memory_api.py` to use the same fallback path for `/quartz-static/*`.

- **Telegram false-success prevention**
  - Added `chat_id` validity flag in `services/telegram/core/config.py`.
  - In `services/telegram/core/bot.py`:
    - `/send` and `/test` now **fail with `412`** if bot token exists but `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` is missing/invalid.
    - `/health` now returns `chat_id_configured`.
  - In `services/scheduler/core/scheduler.py`:
    - Scheduler now checks Telegram notify response status + JSON `ok` before logging notification success.
    - This removes “success” when Telegram actually rejected delivery.

- **Docs**
  - Updated `README.md` and `CHANGELOG.md` to reflect this behavior change.

### Why your current Telegram test looked “successful”

- Scheduler previously marked notify as success without checking Telegram response body/status.
- Also, your `.env` appears to have `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` set to a bot-like id value; for delivery you need the **target chat/channel id** (group/channel/user destination), not the bot token id.

### Verification run

- `services/telegram` tests: pass.
- Python syntax compile for all edited Python files: pass.
- `services/scheduler` test suite currently fails on an existing import-path test harness issue (`ModuleNotFoundError: scheduler`) unrelated to these logic changes.

### Next step for you

- Set a real destination `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` in `.env`, then restart:
  - `telegram`
  - `scheduler`
  - `hub-memory-api`

If you want, I can run through a quick live validation sequence (`/health`, `/test`, then manual scheduler trigger) with you next.

</user_query>

 ### Brain + Telegram — Quartz source alignment and strict notification checks (2026-04-28)

+- `hub-memory-api` now mounts `BRAIN_PATH` as `rw` (not `ro`) because `hub_settings.json` is written under `MEMORY_ROOT/kanban`.

 - Scheduler now validates Telegram notify HTTP responses (`raise_for_status` + JSON `ok`) before logging `job.notified`, avoiding false “success” when delivery failed.

content.text:
Validation run complete. Current status:

- **Quartz/Brain reflection:** ✅ fixed  
  - `GET http://127.0.0.1:8091/settings` now returns:
    - `active_brain_memory: /mnt/c/Users/lgiron/Documents/developer-brain`
  - `GET /projects` returns your real Obsidian project files (not empty anymore).

- **Telegram test delivery:** ❌ still failing  
  - `POST http://127.0.0.1:8094/test` returns `{"ok": false, "error": "internal error"}`
  - Telegram container log shows exact cause:  
    `Forbidden: bots can't send messages to bots`
  - Health endpoint currently reports `chat_id: 8628096218`, which is still a **bot id-like value**, not a target chat/channel id.

- **Scheduler “success” signal:** ✅ behavior corrected  
  - Triggered `hub-refresh` via Hub API.
  - Scheduler now logs a proper error on Telegram failure:
    - `HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error`
    - `job.notify_error ...`
  - So no more false-positive notify success.

### What to set now

Use a real destination id for `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`:
- private chat: your numeric user chat id
- group/channel: usually negative (often `-100...` for channels/supergroups)

Then restart:
- `telegram`
- `scheduler`

If you want, I can do one more immediate re-check right after you paste/update that exact numeric `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`.
I’ll create one commit with all current tracked/untracked changes in the repo and then confirm clean status. I’m first pulling status/diff/log to craft a proper commit message.
